name: git-advanced
description: Advanced Git operations and workflows including interactive rebasing, conflict resolution, history manipulation, bisecting for bugs, cherry-picking, reflog recovery, and branch management strategies. Use for: (1) Interactive rebasing and commit cleanup, (2) Complex merge conflict resolution, (3) Git bisect for bug hunting, (4) History rewriting and cleanup, (5) Branch strategy implementation (Git Flow, trunk-based), (6) Recovering lost commits with reflog
capabilities: Interactive rebasing with squash, reorder, edit, and split operations
Strategic merge conflict resolution using ours, theirs, and manual approaches
Git bisect for binary search bug hunting across commit history
Commit recovery using reflog and fsck for lost work restoration
Branch strategy implementation for Git Flow, trunk-based, and GitHub Flow workflows
Helper script for branch cleanup, stash management, and history visualization
author: AutumnsGrove
securitySummary: This is a documentation and helper script skill for Git operations. Static findings flagged 616 patterns as potentially malicious, but all are false positives: the patterns appear in markdown documentation showing Git command examples (git rebase, git bisect, etc.), not executable code. The helper script (git_helper.sh) provides branch cleanup, conflict resolution, and rebase assistance utilities with user confirmations before destructive operations. No network calls to external endpoints, no credential access, and no persistence mechanisms. Safe for publishing.
valueStatement: Complex Git operations like interactive rebasing, conflict resolution, and history rewriting can be error-prone without guidance. This skill provides step-by-step workflows, examples, and helper scripts to safely navigate advanced version control scenarios.
userTitle: Master advanced Git operations and workflows
limitations: Does not execute Git operations automatically - provides guidance and examples only
Does not access or modify repositories outside the current working directory
Does not integrate with remote services or CI/CD pipelines directly
User must confirm all destructive operations like branch deletion and force push

useCases.title: Clean up commit history
useCases.description: Squash WIP commits, reorder changes, and create readable commit history before code review

useCases.title: Implement branch strategies
useCases.description: Set up Git Flow, trunk-based development, or custom branching workflows for team coordination

useCases.title: Recover lost commits
useCases.description: Use reflog and recovery techniques to restore accidentally deleted branches and commits

promptTemplates.title: Rebase assistance
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me use interactive rebase to squash the last 5 commits into one clean commit with a descriptive message

promptTemplates.title: Conflict resolution
promptTemplates.prompt: Guide me through resolving merge conflicts between my feature branch and main, including when to use ours versus theirs

promptTemplates.title: Bug hunting
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to use git bisect to find which commit introduced a bug that exists now but was not present in version 1.0.0

promptTemplates.title: Recovery
promptTemplates.prompt: I accidentally reset my branch and lost important commits. Help me recover them using reflog

bestPractices: Always create a backup branch before rebasing or rewriting history
Never rebase commits that have been pushed to shared branches
Use git reflog to recover from mistakes - it preserves 30-90 days of HEAD history
antiPatterns: Rebasing public branches that others depend on
Skipping commit message review during interactive rebase
Ignoring conflict markers without understanding both sides

faq.question: Does this skill work with all Git hosting platforms?
faq.answer: Yes, the commands work with GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and self-hosted Git servers.

faq.question: What are the limits of git bisect?
faq.answer: Requires a test that can return pass/fail. Best for clear bug scenarios with known good and bad commits.

faq.question: Can I use this with GitHub CLI or GitLab CLI?
faq.answer: Yes, the Git commands are compatible with any Git CLI regardless of hosting platform.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using the helper script?
faq.answer: The script requires user confirmation before destructive actions and only operates in the current repository.

faq.question: Why did my rebase fail?
faq.answer: Most failures are from conflicts. Abort with git rebase --abort, resolve conflicts, then continue or start over.

faq.question: How is this different from standard Git documentation?
faq.answer: This skill provides interactive guidance, workflow examples, and a helper script that streamlines complex operations.
